backlog.title: Paged Delivery for Oversize Files (PRD §5.5)
backlog.description: Add automatic paged delivery to the existing #@file extension so that text files exceeding the model's remaining context budget are delivered as a head block plus a paging directive (instructing the model to read the rest via the read tool) instead of being injected whole. The existing MVP is fully implemented; this delta adds §5.5 only. Images, binaries, missing/directory handling, strip-#@, and autocomplete are unchanged. All Pi API claims (getContextUsage, ctx.model shape, estimateTokens, read tool default limit) are VERIFIED against v0.80.7 — see plan/002_0ac3eb160af7/architecture/pi_api_verification.md.

milestones.title: Implement & Validate Paged Delivery
milestones.description: Implement budget-aware inline-vs-paged branching in injectFiles, update the handler notify to report mode, update JSDoc (Mode A), and sync the README (Mode B). The change is scoped to file-injector.ts (single file) and file-injector.test.mjs (test harness). No new files, no new dependencies.

tasks.title: Budget-Aware Paged Delivery Implementation
tasks.description: Add the 5 paged-delivery constants, the formatPagedDirectiveBlock helper, widen injectFiles's ctx and return types, implement the remaining-context budget computation with O-1/O-2 fallbacks, insert the inline-vs-paged decision branch at the text-file path, wire multi-file remaining subtraction, add the paged counter, and update the handler notify to report whole-vs-paged mode. Update JSDoc on injectFiles and the factory function (Mode A docs).

subtasks.title: Add paged-delivery constants, formatPagedDirectiveBlock helper, and widen injectFiles types
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: All 5 constants are defined in PRD §5.5 ('Constants (defaults to pin)'). The formatPagedDirectiveBlock helper emits a <file name="abs"> directive block using the em-dash (U+2014) convention from the existing formatBinaryBlock/formatEmptyImageBlock helpers (see architecture/implementation_insertion_points.md §2). The ctx type must be widened so the budget computation in S2 can access getContextUsage() and ctx.model — both VERIFIED on ExtensionContext (architecture/pi_api_verification.md §2, §4). ContextUsage = { tokens: number | null; contextWindow: number; percent: number | null } — tokens is nullable.
2. INPUT: The existing file-injector.ts at its current HEAD state. Constants FILE_INJECT_RE (L8), MIME_BY_EXT (L9-16), TRAILING_PUNCT (L17). Format helpers at L90-112. injectFiles signature at L122-126 with ctx: { cwd: string } and return Promise<{ text, images, injected }>. Two return sites: early-return at L209 and final-return at L217.
3. LOGIC:
  a. After TRAILING_PUNCT (L17), add 5 constants:
     const PAGED_THRESHOLD = 0.6; // inject whole if fileCost <= PAGED_THRESHOLD * remaining
     const MARGIN = 8192;          // safety bytes subtracted from remaining context
     const HEAD_BYTES = 8192;      // head block size (~2000 lines, matches read tool DEFAULT_MAX_LINES)
     const DEFAULT_WINDOW = 200000;   // fallback context window (vestigial — usage.contextWindow preferred)
     const DEFAULT_RESERVE = 8192;    // fallback for ctx.model?.maxTokens when model is absent
  b. After formatEmptyImageBlock (L112), add:
     export function formatPagedDirectiveBlock(abs: string, totalBytes: number): string — emits '<file name="' + abs + '"><large file \u2014 estimated ' + totalBytes + ' bytes; first ' + HEAD_BYTES + ' bytes injected above. Use the read tool to load the rest: offset:0, limit:2000, incrementing offset until the entire file is read></file>'
     Use the em-dash (U+2014, \u2014) for visual consistency with formatBinaryBlock.
  c. Widen injectFiles ctx param from { cwd: string } to:
     { cwd: string; getContextUsage?: () => { tokens: number | null; contextWindow: number; percent: number | null } | undefined; model?: { contextWindow: number; maxTokens: number } | undefined }
     Both new fields are OPTIONAL so the existing test mock FIX = { cwd: TMPDIR } still satisfies the type.
  d. Widen injectFiles return type to add paged: number:
     Promise<{ text: string; images: ImageContent[]; injected: number; paged: number }>
  e. Update BOTH return sites to include paged: 0 (early-return L209 and final-return L217). No behavioral change — paged is always 0 in this subtask.
  f. Update injectFiles JSDoc (L114-121): replace the 'no truncation, no word/byte cap' framing with 'the whole file always reaches the model: injected inline when it fits remaining context, paged via the model read tool when it does not (PRD §5.5)'. Reference §5.5.
4. OUTPUT: file-injector.ts with new constants, formatPagedDirectiveBlock (exported), widened types, and updated JSDoc. Runtime behavior is UNCHANGED — all files still inject whole, paged is always 0. The existing test harness must pass without modification (formatPagedDirectiveBlock is not yet called; the module-surface sanity check at test L117-124 must be updated to include the new export).
5. DOCS: Mode A — update JSDoc on injectFiles to describe inline-vs-paged per §5.5. No README change here (Mode B is handled in P1.M1.T2). Update the test module-surface assertion list (file-injector.test.mjs L117-124) to include formatPagedDirectiveBlock in the exported-symbols sanity check.

subtasks.title: Budget computation and inline-vs-paged decision branch in injectFiles
Handler notify reports whole-vs-paged mode; update factory JSDoc
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: ctx.ui.notify signature is (message: string, type?: 'info'|'warning'|'error'): void (VERIFIED). ctx.hasUI is boolean (VERIFIED). The handler at L251 calls injectFiles and destructures { text, images, injected }. The notify at L254 is the single mode-reporting line, guarded on ctx.hasUI. The factory JSDoc at L220-244 contains a paragraph claiming 'No limits, no config: the whole file is injected every time — no truncation' which is now factually wrong per §5.5. See architecture/implementation_insertion_points.md §4.
2. INPUT: injectFiles now returns { text, images, injected, paged } from S2. The handler at L251 currently destructures only { text, images, injected }. The notify at L254 currently reads: if (ctx.hasUI) ctx.ui.notify(`#@ injected ${injected} ${injected === 1 ? 'file' : 'files'}`, 'info').
3. LOGIC:
  a. At L251, destructure paged: const { text, images, injected, paged } = await injectFiles(event.text, event.images ?? [], ctx);
  b. Replace the notify (L254) with mode-aware logic:
     const whole = injected - paged;
     const msg = paged > 0
       ? `#@ injected ${whole} whole, ${paged} paged`
       : `#@ injected ${injected} ${injected === 1 ? 'file' : 'files'}`;
     if (ctx.hasUI) ctx.ui.notify(msg, 'info');
     The paged === 0 path preserves the existing pluralized message style. The paged > 0 path reports whole and paged counts separately (PRD §5.5 Notify; delta_prd PD-2).
  c. The 'if (!injected) return { action: continue }' guard at L252 is UNCHANGED — injected still counts all delivered files (whole + paged), so injected === 0 still means nothing was delivered.
  d. Update the factory JSDoc (L220-244): replace the paragraph beginning 'No limits, no config: the whole file is injected every time — no truncation' with: 'The whole file always reaches the model: injected inline when it fits the remaining context budget, or delivered as a head block plus a paging directive (instructing the model to read the rest via the read tool) when it exceeds it (PRD §5.5). The budget is derived from the active model context window and current usage — no user-facing config. Images are downscaled (2000x2000) because providers reject oversized images.' Keep the rest of the JSDoc (loop prevention, steering skip, never-throw) unchanged.
4. OUTPUT: Handler with mode-aware notify and accurate factory JSDoc. When paged > 0, the notify says '#@ injected N whole, M paged'; otherwise it preserves the existing '#@ injected N file(s)' style.
5. MOCKING: Tests verify the notify via the existing makeMockCtx helper which records ctx.ui.notify calls. Test: (a) all-whole prompt → notify says 'N file(s)' (existing style); (b) mixed prompt with PAGED_FIX → notify says 'W whole, M paged'; (c) all-paged prompt → notify says '0 whole, M paged'; (d) hasUI === false → notify never called (existing headless guard). Test the handler-level behavior using captureHandler() (test harness L142-147) with crafted payloads.
6. DOCS: Mode A — update factory JSDoc (L220-244) to replace the 'no truncation' paragraph with the paged-delivery description. This is the code-adjacent doc touch that rides with the work.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B)
tasks.description: Update README.md to reflect the new paged-delivery behavior. Three sections are stale: 'Why' (line 3), 'What gets injected' table (line 41), and 'Limits' (line 72, 'No size gate' bullet). This is a cross-cutting doc sync that only makes sense once all implementation is in place.

subtasks.title: Update README.md Why, What gets injected, and Limits sections for paged delivery
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md current content is documented verbatim in architecture/test_and_docs_analysis.md §2. Three sections are factually stale after the paged-delivery delta. The README also has a '#@ versus @' section (L78-83) ending with 'Use @ or the read tool when you want part of a file or a size limit' — review whether this needs a touch (the distinction still holds: #@ delivers the whole file via inline-or-paged; @ and read are for partial reads, but the 'size limit' framing is now blurred since #@ handles oversize automatically).
2. INPUT: The implemented paged-delivery behavior from P1.M1.T1 (all three subtasks). README.md at its current HEAD (83 lines, content in architecture/test_and_docs_analysis.md §2.1).
3. LOGIC:
  a. 'Why' section (line 3 + lines 5-9): Reword the opening promise. Current L3 says 'with no size limit and no configuration'. Replace with: 'the whole file always reaches the model — injected whole when it fits the remaining context, paged via the read tool when it doesn\'t — with no configuration.' Current L7-9 says '#@ always injects the entire file, in every context'. Reword to: '#@ always delivers the entire file to the model, in every context: the editor, a pi -p one-shot, and RPC. When the file fits the remaining context it is injected whole; when it exceeds the budget it is delivered as a head block plus a paging directive that the model reads through.'
  b. 'What gets injected' table (line 41, Text row): Current: '| Text (.ts, .md, .json, .log, etc.) | Entire contents injected, no truncation. |'. Replace with: '| Text (.ts, .md, .json, .log, etc.) | Entire contents injected when they fit remaining context. Oversize files are delivered as a head block plus a paging directive — the model reads the rest via the read tool. Never silently truncated. |'
  c. 'Limits' section (line 72, 'No size gate' bullet): Current: '- **No size gate.** #@ on a 50 MB file injects 50 MB and can overflow the model\'s context. For large files, use Pi\'s read tool with offset and limit.'. Replace with: '- **No size knob.** #@ has no user-facing size setting. Oversize text files are delivered as a head block (first ~8 KB) plus a paging directive; the model reads the rest via the read tool. The model never holds a file larger than its context window all at once — that is a property of the medium, not of this extension.'
  d. Review the '#@ versus @' closing section (L78-83): 'Use @ or the read tool when you want part of a file or a size limit.' The 'size limit' clause is now misleading since #@ handles oversize automatically. Reword to: 'Use #@ when you want all of a file. Use @ or the read tool when you want to browse or search without loading the whole file.'
4. OUTPUT: README.md with updated Why, What gets injected, Limits, and #@-versus-@ sections. All three stale sections reconciled with the paged-delivery behavior. No other README sections change (Install, Usage, Syntax are unaffected).
5. DOCS: Mode B — this IS the changeset-level documentation sync task. No code changes here; this subtask only touches README.md.
